executiveSummary.text:
Oklo is one of the cleanest listed ways to express the view that AI-era electricity demand will force the market to take advanced nuclear seriously. The company’s appeal is not just “nuclear”, it is power for compute. That positioning matters because it links Oklo directly to one of the strongest structural demand stories in the market.
            The live price is $46.59, with a market cap of roughly $8.09bn. It is still a loss-making company with EPS -0.72, and the stock is down 76.0% from th

businessModel.text:
Oklo is developing advanced microreactors designed to supply reliable, low-carbon power where uptime and energy density matter. The commercial target set is attractive: data centres, industrial sites, remote applications and other environments where firm power can command premium economics.
            The company’s strength is narrative alignment. If hyperscalers, data-centre developers or industrial power buyers start securing future dedicated nuclear capacity, Oklo is positioned to benefit. The weakness is that this still needs to move from strategic interest to deployable, financeable commercial reality.

financialSnapshot.text:
Live price: $46.59
Market cap: $8.09bn
EPS: -0.72
52 week range: $19.89 to $193.84
3 month share price move: -52.3%

The market cap remains large relative to present fundamentals. That means investors are still capitalising future strategic value, not current earnings power. It also means downside remains real if timelines slip.

recentCatalysts.text:
Regulatory progress has helped keep the licensing pathway credible
              The stock remains tightly linked to the AI infrastructure power-demand story
              Investor interest remains elevated despite the sharp pullback from prior highs
              Oklo continues to sit near the centre of the market’s advanced nuclear speculation basket

thesisEvaluation.text:
Bull (30%)
                Oklo secures stronger counterparties, continues to clear milestones and becomes one of the market’s preferred listed beneficiaries of AI-linked power demand.
              
              
                Base (45%)
                The story remains attractive, but progress is gradual and the stock continues to trade on thematic sentiment more than on current economics.
              
              
                Bear (25%)
                Commercial adoption takes longer than hoped, regulatory timelines stretch and the market cuts the premium attached to the AI-power nuclear theme.
              
            
            Oklo does not need to be profitable tomorrow to work as a stock, but it does need to keep investors convinced that it can become strategically important before capital patience fades.

risks.risk: Execution risk: deployment remains ahead of the company, not behind it.
Licensing risk: advanced nuclear pathways remain slow and can surprise to the downside.
Valuation risk: the stock can compress sharply when expectations move faster than fundamentals.
Financing risk: pre-profit businesses remain exposed to capital market conditions.
Narrative concentration: much of the bull case depends on AI-power urgency staying strong.

recommendation.text:
HOLD — SPECULATIVE.
            Oklo is the more compelling speculative equity than NuScale because the market story is cleaner and more current. But it still belongs in the speculative bucket. The right stance is to own it only if you are deliberately buying future optionality, not near-term business certainty.

entryExit.text:
$40-$45: attractive area for speculative adds if the AI-power thesis remains intact
              $46-$60: fair hold range with performance tied to milestone delivery
              $70-$85: sensible trim zone if the market prices hype ahead of fundamentals
            
          

          
            Conviction Trend
            Latest conviction: 61/100. Trend versus prior report: Flat.
